words.definition: A household or family, especially in the context of a large family estate or lineage.
words.example: I want to talk to you!” Doors and windows opened into the yard all around.
words.exampleText: I want to talk to you!” Doors and windows opened into the yard all around.

words.definition: The name of an important family or spell-house in Caprona.
words.example: They agreed that it probably did, and wanted to wait to see a Petrocchi come out.
words.exampleText: They agreed that it probably did, and wanted to wait to see a Petrocchi come out.

words.definition: A tall structure or part of a building, often at a corner or gate.
words.example: The Casa Petrocchi had four golden-stone towers, one at each corner.
words.exampleText: The Casa Petrocchi had four golden-stone towers, one at each corner.

words.definition: The external upper covering of a building.
words.example: Each roof was covered with red pan-tiles, just like their own roofs.
words.exampleText: Each roof was covered with red pan-tiles, just like their own roofs.

words.definition: An opening in a fence or wall that allows entrance or exit.
words.example: Casa Montana only had one tower, over the gate.
words.exampleText: Casa Montana only had one tower, over the gate.

words.definition: A corridor or balcony overlooking a space, often with railings.
words.example: Rosa ran up the steps to the gallery, shouting, “Lucia! Lucia, where are you?”
words.exampleText: Rosa ran up the steps to the gallery, shouting, “Lucia! Lucia, where are you?”

words.definition: A barrier made of rails, used as a guard or support on stairs or balconies.
words.example: The gallery, with its wooden railings and pan-tiled roof.
words.exampleText: The gallery, with its wooden railings and pan-tiled roof.

words.definition: A magic charm or incantation used to produce a magical effect.
words.example: Everyone in the Casa Montana worked very hard on learning spells.
words.exampleText: Everyone in the Casa Montana worked very hard on learning spells.

words.definition: A period of learning or teaching about a particular subject.
words.example: I’ve been ready for your lessons for half an hour or more!
words.exampleText: I’ve been ready for your lessons for half an hour or more!

words.definition: A person who plays or teaches music.
words.example: Old Niccolo said Elizabeth was the best musician in Caprona.
words.exampleText: Old Niccolo said Elizabeth was the best musician in Caprona.

words.definition: The power to influence events using supernatural forces or spells.
words.example: Paolo had been born with an instinct for magic.
words.exampleText: Paolo had been born with an instinct for magic.

words.definition: A child of one's aunt or uncle.
words.example: Paolo admired his cousin, dashing Rinaldo.
words.exampleText: Paolo admired his cousin, dashing Rinaldo.

words.definition: An institution for educating children or students.
words.example: Paolo had just started school.
words.exampleText: Paolo had just started school.

words.definition: A single teaching session or unit of instruction.
words.example: Paolo was as quick at ordinary lessons as at spells.
words.exampleText: Paolo was as quick at ordinary lessons as at spells.

words.definition: A group of related people living together or connected by blood.
words.example: In the Casa Montana, if anyone was in distress, it fetched the rest of the family.
words.exampleText: In the Casa Montana, if anyone was in distress, it fetched the rest of the family.

words.definition: A state of extreme worry, sadness, or pain.
words.example: If anyone was in distress, it always fetched the rest of the family.
words.exampleText: If anyone was in distress, it always fetched the rest of the family.

words.definition: A room or place where books are kept for reading or study.
words.example: Old Niccolo was in the warm mildewy library consulting a book.
words.exampleText: Old Niccolo was in the warm mildewy library consulting a book.

words.definition: A set of pages bound together containing text or images.
words.example: Aunt Francesca was consulting over a book with Old Niccolo.
words.exampleText: Aunt Francesca was consulting over a book with Old Niccolo.

words.definition: Something kept hidden or known only to a few.
words.example: Each book had a brass lock so that family secrets could not be stolen.
words.exampleText: Each book had a brass lock so that family secrets could not be stolen.

words.definition: A child of one’s aunt or uncle.
words.example: Five other cousins were also copying spells in the Scriptorium.
words.exampleText: Five other cousins were also copying spells in the Scriptorium.

words.definition: To form letters or words on a surface, usually paper.
words.example: Rinaldo’s pen raced, writing spells quickly.
words.exampleText: Rinaldo’s pen raced, writing spells quickly.

words.definition: A teaching period on a subject.
words.example: Aunt Maria said she’s been ready for your lessons for half an hour.
words.exampleText: Aunt Maria said she’s been ready for your lessons for half an hour.

words.definition: To hold someone tightly in your arms, usually to show affection.
words.example: Aunt Maria rushed at Paolo and hugged him.
words.exampleText: Aunt Maria rushed at Paolo and hugged him.

words.definition: To give lessons or instruct someone.
words.example: Elizabeth taught the boys music.
words.exampleText: Elizabeth taught the boys music.

words.definition: To disagree or exchange reasons for or against something.
words.example: They did not like to argue with Rosa.
words.exampleText: They did not like to argue with Rosa.

words.definition: To pull someone or something with effort or force.
words.example: Rosa said she thought that would be most unwise, and pulled them away.
words.exampleText: Rosa said she thought that would be most unwise, and pulled them away.
